First Name	Ally
Last Name	Keyes
Email	allysonhkeyes@gmail.com
Current Status	Recent Graduate (0-12 months)
Job Type Preference	Internship
3. What types of roles are you targeting right now?	Legal Internship

Consumer, Retail & Lifestyle	Fashion and Apparel Beauty and Personal Care Food and Beverage Sports and Entertainment Hospitality
Specific companies you are particularly interested in (max 10)	NBCU, Universal Music Group, Moody's Corporation, Sony, Jones Day, Capri Holdings
5. Are there any roles or industries you D O N O T want?	big law (I don't think)
How open are you to non-obvious entry points?	Very open
6. Location preferences	NYC, LA
7. Timeline for starting work	Summer 2026
8. What do you believe are your strongest skills right now?	Communication, outgoing personality, knowledge of the media industry
9. What about your job search gives you most concern?	not standing out amongst competition
10. How do you prefer feedback delivered?	Direct & Blunt (don't sugarcoat it)

Edit and adapt messaging to different audiences, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and alignment with institutional standards. Review and approve posts for compliance with University guidelines. Law Offices of Craig Donoff, P.A., Boca Raton, FL Legal Intern, May 2025 – August 2025 Drafted and reviewed estate planning documents, including wills, trusts, and powers of attorney, under attorney supervision. Attended and took notes during client meetings, maintaining confidentiality and observing professional attorney-client interactions. Conduced legal research on probate procedures, trust administration, and estate tax issues to support attorney casework. Organized and updated client files and legal records, ensuring compliance with firm protocols and regulatory standards. Elevate Advertising Agency, Gainesville, FL Communications and Media Director, August 2022 – April 2025 Directed communications strategy for a 40-member student-run ad agency, overseeing client messaging, media relations, and brand positioning. Supervised cross-functional teams, ensuring compliance with deadlines, clear delegation of responsibilities, and high-quality deliverables for clients. Krinzman, Huss, Lubetsky, Feldman & Hotte, Attorneys at Law, Miami, FL Legal Intern, June 2024 – March 2025 Observed civil litigation proceedings, gaining firsthand insight into courtroom procedures and case strategy. Researched case law, statues, and legal precedents to support litigation teams in drafting memoranda and case briefs. Compiled and organized legal documents and case materials for attorney review and use in client meetings, negotiations, and court presentations. UF Panhellenic Council, Gainesville, FL Director of Website Design and Content, January 2024 – December 2024 Drafted and designed the website's monthly newsletter. Increased viewership by 208%. Communicated with the council’s 18 sorority chapters, council leadership, and national Panhellenic leaders to stay current on relevant updates and announcements to promote on the website. SKILLS AND INTERESTS Languages: Spanish (speaking, reading, and writing) Interests: Social media campaigns, theme parks, art & design, entertainment studio production
Cover Letter or Other Writing Samples	December 20, 2025 Hiring Committee Universal Music Group Re: 2026 Summer Internship: Business & Legal Affairs Opportunities (NY) Dear Universal Music Group Internship Team, Universal Music Group sits at the intersection of creativity, culture, and complex business operations, which is exactly where my interests lie. As a first year law student with a background in advertising and communications, I am excited to apply for the 2026 Summer Internship Program and to be considered for the New York and Florida based Business and Legal Affairs opportunities. What draws me to UMG is the breadth of legal and business issues handled in house. From artist agreements and clearances to marketing initiatives, digital content, and technology driven deals, these roles reflect how law operates as a strategic partner rather than a purely reactive function. I am eager to gain exposure to how Business and Legal Affairs teams balance creativity, risk, and commercial realities while supporting some of the most influential artists and catalogs in the world. Before law school, I earned a degree in Advertising, where I developed a strong foundation in brand strategy, messaging, and audience awareness. That experience shapes how I think about reputation, intellectual property, and the downstream impact of contracts and business decisions in a consumer facing industry like music. Through my law school education and internships, I have gained legal experience that required research, drafting, document review, and attention to detail, while learning how legal teams collaborate with non legal stakeholders to move projects forward efficiently and responsibly. I am particularly interested in the New York based Business and Legal Affairs roles because of their focus on artist relations, deal structuring, clearance, and cross functional collaboration. I bring strong writing and organizational skills, comfort working independently, and a genuine curiosity about how legal frameworks support the release, promotion, and protection of creative work. I am also interested in the Florida based UMLE programs where I can utilize my Spanish language proficiency and knowledge of Latin label markets. I believe I would be a strong addition to UMG’s internship program because I bring a combination of communication fluency, legal curiosity, and respect for the creative process. I am eager to learn, open to feedback, and excited by the opportunity to contribute meaningfully while developing a deeper understanding of Business and Legal Affairs within the music industry. Thank you for your time and consideration. Sincerely, Allyson Keyes J.D. Candidate, 2028 | University of Miami School of Law allysonhkeyes@gmail.com | (561) 955-0395 Allyson Keyes (561) 955-0395 allysonhkeyes@gmail.com December 17, 2025 Hiring Committee Capri Holdings Re: 2026 Corporate Summer Internship Dear Capri Holdings Internship Program Team, Capri Holdings represents the intersection of creativity, global business, and brand integrity, which is exactly where my interests lie. As a first-year law student with a background in advertising and communications, I am excited by the opportunity to gain hands-on experience within a global luxury organization and to better understand how corporate functions support iconic brands at scale. I am particularly interested in opportunities within Legal, especially Employment Law and ESG, where law, people, and brand values closely intersect. Before law school, I earned a degree in Advertising, where I developed a strong understanding of brand strategy, consumer perception, and the importance of consistency and trust in public-facing companies. That background shapes how I approach legal issues today, especially in industries like luxury fashion where reputation, culture, and global operations are deeply connected. Since beginning law school, I have built practical legal experience through internships that involved research, drafting, and supporting attorneys across a range of matters, while learning how legal teams operate as strategic partners within larger organizations. What draws me to Capri Holdings is the company’s emphasis on craftsmanship, accountability, and innovation across its brand portfolio. I am especially interested in how legal and ESG teams help guide corporate responsibility, workplace standards, and ethical decision-making in a global retail environment. I enjoy work that requires organization, clear communication, and collaboration across departments, and I am eager to learn how legal frameworks support both creative teams and operational goals. I believe I would be a strong addition to the Capri Holdings internship program because I bring curiosity, adaptability, and a genuine enthusiasm for learning how a global fashion company operates behind the scenes. I am detail-oriented, comfortable managing multiple priorities, and motivated by environments that value accountability and thoughtful problem-solving. Most importantly, I bring a strong respect for the role legal teams play in protecting brand legacy while supporting growth and innovation. Thank you for your time and consideration. I would welcome the opportunity to contribute to Capri Holdings this summer and to learn from professionals shaping the future of luxury retail. Sincerely, Allyson Keyes J.D. Candidate, 2028 | University of Miami School of Law allysonhkeyes@gmail.com | (561) 955-0395 December 18, 2025 Hiring Committee Paramount Legal Affairs Re: Summer 2026 Legal Affairs (JD/LLM) Internship Dear Paramount Legal Affairs Internship Team, Paramount sits at the center of culture, content, and conversation, and the legal work that supports that ecosystem is exactly what draws me to this internship. As a first-year law student at the University of Miami School of Law with a background in advertising and media, I am excited by the opportunity to learn how legal and business affairs team...

jobText:
Job description
Description:

Business and Legal Affairs interns will gain experience in:
• Drafting of basic agreements, amendments, and other legal documents
• Analysis of content license agreements in connection with company-mandated requirements
• Analysis of various agreements for input of rights information into the department’s rights management system
• Review and revision of form agreements.

Qualifications:
• Desired Backgrounds: Must be enrolled in a Juris Doctor program at an accredited law school and have completed at least one year
• Classes or experience in copyright and contracts law preferred. Trademark law also a plus.
• Desired Characteristics: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, knowledge of MS Office (Word, Excel and Outlook), strong organizational skills, ability to handle multiple tasks in fast paced environment, attention to detail and a sense of confidence and discretion; knowledge of entertainment and copyright law helpful; must be able to work both in a team environment and independently.
• Must be authorized to work in the United States without visa sponsorship by NBCUniversal
• Needs to be able to work on-site in New York, NY or Miami, FL
• Internships at NBCUniversal are paid and do not require course credit
• Deadline: Interested applicants are encouraged to apply by February 15, 2019
• Summer Program Dates: June - August 2019
